notes: v1.6.92 — MICRO-CUT (NOW-tab coherence + ring label). Two surgical items, nothing else touched. (1) RING LABEL: the TODAY completion ring's label 'CONFIDENCE' → 'COMPLETE' at all three non-blocked thresholds (green ≥90 / cyan ≥50 / gold <50); the BLOCKED branch is unchanged. Fixes the misleading name (the ring shows completion %, not a confidence score) and the text/ring overlap. The unrelated Optic-Seating Confidence Score feature was deliberately left untouched (only the 3 ringLabel assignment sites changed). (2) NOW-TAB COHERENCE: today_render() previously summed counts across ALL active deployments and labeled the panel with whichever was updated last, so the TODAY counts/bar/ring could disagree with the ACTIVE BUILD card (which shows a single deployment). The whole TODAY panel now tracks ONE deployment — resolved deploy_getActive() first, with the most-recently-updated active deployment ('current') as fallback — so counts, progress bar, completion ring, name, rack rail, blockers, next-action and burndown all agree with the ACTIVE BUILD hero. Implementation: the aggregate active.forEach was replaced by a single deploy_computeDeployRollup on the resolved deployment; mostRecentDeploy is reassigned to that deployment so every downstream consumer follows with no further edits; blocker cards are scoped to it. Empty-state and the ACTIVE BUILD card (activeContext_get) are unchanged. VERIFIED: node-checked (2 inline scripts + sw.js all OK); diff confined entirely to today_render() (every hunk in lines 17617-17743) so the style block is provably untouched — style-block brace count 2494, identical to HEAD v1.6.91 (NOTE: the runbook's '3178' baseline is stale — HEAD is also 2494). Browser end-to-end pending operator device walkthrough (Runbook Step 2). Built in Claude Code on 5825cfb (v1.6.91); web-Claude does post-merge parity review (one-builder rule). Three stamps at v1.6.92.
